title: PCA and SVD / truncated SVD
key_concepts: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) are the workhorses of linear dimensionality reduction.
PCA finds orthogonal directions (principal components) that maximize the variance of the data.
SVD factors the original data matrix into three components.
Truncated SVD is specifically used for sparse matrices where mean-centering (required by standard PCA) would destroy sparsity.
equations: SVD factorization: $X = U \Sigma V^T$, where $U$ and $V$ are orthogonal matrices and $\Sigma$ is a diagonal matrix of singular values.
PCA projection: $X_{reduced} = X W_k$, projecting data onto the top $k$ principal components.
examples: Mention applying Truncated SVD to sparse TF-IDF text matrices (connecting to Ch 11).
relations: Truncated SVD connects to Ch 11 text processing.
